title: machine: share virtiofs systemd unit generation
body:
Make GenerateSystemDFilesForVirtiofsMounts an agnostic function and move the code from apple to pkg/machine/volume_systemd.go.

MountVolumesToVM  is now a no-op matching the AppleHV and LibKrun behaviour.

Continues from PR: https://github.com/podman-container-tools/podman/pull/28736

Fixes: https://redhat.atlassian.net/browse/RUN-4742

<!--
Write `None` if there are no user-facing changes, otherwise enter your release note below.
Include "action required" if users need to take action when upgrading.

```release-note
Mount volumes in machines via systemd. Breaking change on Linux: existing podman machines will not work correctly. Remove and rerun podman machine init
```

comments.body: Note, with this change the existing machines will have to be stopped, removed and recreated, since they won't have the correct systemd files at init time. Is it considered a breaking change or just workaroundable? @baude @Honny1

> Note, with this change the existing machines will have to be stopped, removed and recreated, since they won't have the correct systemd files at init time. Is it considered a breaking change or just workaroundable? @baude @Honny1

Given it is "only" for linux I think it should be fine and as long as we merge it before 6.0 we can just document it as breaking change and be done with it. So please update the release notes block to document this change
cool. rebased onto the new actions and updated the release note

Ok No New Tests label no longer works on re-run, it is like I assumed and uses the original github context and does not refresh labels. I file an issue.

cc @kolyshkin
Created https://github.com/podman-container-tools/podman/issues/28838, @inknos if you repush I think it will work though so please do that for now.
